channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Intel’s anticipated $28 billion chip factories in Ohio are delayed until 2030
item.description: Intel‘s promised $28 billion chip fabrication plants in Ohio are facing further delays, with the first factory in New Albany expected to not be completed until 2030, local media outlet The Columbus Dispatch reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Hollywood’s obsession with AI-enabled ‘perfection’ is making movies less human
item.description: The notion of authenticity in the movies has moved a step beyond the merely realistic. More and more, expensive and time-consuming fixes to minor issues of screen realism have become the work of statistical data renderings—the visual or aural products of generative artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: A.S. Hamrah, Fast Company

item.title: NASA’s SPHEREx Space Telescope will create the world’s most complete sky survey
item.description: The sky is about to get a lot clearer. NASA’s latest infrared space telescope, SPHEREx—short for Spectro-Photometer for the History of the Universe, Epoch of Reionization, and Ices Explorer—will assemble the world’s most complete sky survey to better explain how the universe evolved.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas
item.description: Federal use cases typically fall into one of five “swim lanes,” with most impacting customer or citizen services, said the IDC Insight's federal researcher. The post AI and Data Exchange 2025: IDC’s Max Claps on agencies driving AI into mission areas first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is officially retiring Skype, the platform once popular for making calls and messaging, and redirecting users to Teams, the tech giant announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight
item.description: AI presents opportunities for greater efficiency, but GAO chief data officer says its effectiveness hinges on data quality. The post AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: How to get Novacrystal in Monster Hunter Wilds
item.description: Here's where to find Novacrystal in Monster Hunter Wilds, so that you can start crafting better armor sets and weapons.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech, life science companies slash hundreds more Bay Area workers
item.description: Latest layoffs affect workers in the South Bay, East Bay and SF, marking a fresh round of employment setbacks for the vital sectors.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Revlon automatiza las operaciones de final de línea gracias a la robótica
item.description: Revlon se apoya en la tecnología de vanguardia para catapultar la productividad de su planta en el Pla de Santa María, en la provincia de Tarragona.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI priorities: IT leaders emphasize productivity over innovation
item.description: IT leaders are still aiming for the low-hanging fruit when using AI, zeroing in on improving employee productivity rather than more advanced uses such as enabling innovation or expanding revenue, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: IBM closes Hashicorp buy, looks to boost enterprise multicloud and AI automation technology
item.description: In announcing the closure of its $6.4 billion buy of Hashicorp, IBM said its goal is to infuse HashiCorp automation and security technology in every data center possible.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bipartisan senators unveil bill banning DeepSeek on government devices
item.description: A bipartisan group of senators introduced legislation Thursday to ban Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) startup DeepSeek’s products from government devices and networks. The bill, introduced by Sens. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.), Jon Husted (R-Ohio) and Pete Ricketts (R-Neb.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is urging retired air traffic controllers to come back to the workforce amid the nationwide shortage of workers. “There is a shortage of top notch air traffic controllers.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government’s reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple shareholders reject proposal to scrap company's diversity programs
item.description: Apple shareholders rebuffed an attempt to pressure the technology trendsetter into joining President Donald Trump's push to scrub corporate programs designed to diversify its workforce.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX prepares for 8th Starship launch, pending FAA approval
item.description: SpaceX is slated to launch its Starship for the eighth time from Boca Chica, Texas, Friday, though plans could be thwarted if the FAA fails to give the green light.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reform doesn’t come easily to the agency that guards the nuclear weapons stockpile
item.description: The National Nuclear Security Administration is among the agencies trying to reform themselves as it requires certain best practices to be in place. The post Reform doesn’t come easily to the agency that guards the nuclear weapons stockpile first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Five takeaways from Cisco Live EMEA
item.description: Cisco held its European user event in Amsterdam this month, presenting a good mix of industry trends, product innovation, and vision from Cisco as to where they see the market going.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cybersecurity Threats: Infostealer Malware on the Rise in 2024
item.description: Infostealer malware has been on the rise in 2024, with hackers using it to steal credentials, cryptocurrency, and other personal data from millions of users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18 expands cloud-native networking
item.description: Red Hat is updating its OpenShift platform with a series of capabilities that will provide more advanced networking and virtualization functionality for cloud-native deployments. OpenShift is Red Hat’s commercially supported Kubernetes distribution.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 8 ways to make Windows 11 work like Windows 10
item.description: Switching to Windows 11? You’re not alone. With the clock ticking down to Windows 10’s end-of-support date in October, professionals around the globe are making plans — and lots of them are making the upgrade to Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco, Nvidia expand AI partnership to include Silicon One technology
item.description: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to support enterprise AI implementations by tying together Cisco’s Silicon One technology and Nvidia’s Ethernet networking platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google rolls out free version of Gemini Code Assist for individuals
item.description: Google is rolling out a free version of Gemini Code Assist, its tool for code completion, code generation, and code chat, with the aim of helping individual developers improve their productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World
